obligation_text: The authorised representative shall perform the tasks specified in the mandate received from the provider. It shall provide a copy of the mandate to the market surveillance authority upon request. The mandate shall empower the authorised representative to: verify that the EU declaration of conformity and the technical documentation have been drawn up; keep available the documentation for national competent authorities and authorities referred to in Article 74(10); cooperate with competent authorities on any action the latter takes in relation to the high-risk AI system.
scoring_text: Knowledge of EU AI Act authorized representative responsibilities, including conformity documentation verification, documentation availability management, and regulatory authority cooperation procedures for high-risk AI systems.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of EU AI Act authorized representative responsibilities, including conformity documentation verification, documentation availability management, and regulatory authority cooperation procedures for high-risk AI systems.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory compliance requirements, including EU AI Act conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, and documentation requirements for high-risk AI systems.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 22(2-3) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.718, cosine: 0.842). Secondary: RC-S-008 (RC, cosine: 0.836), AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.814).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.2/10 (raw 0.718). Shared vocabulary: [act, ai, conformity, documentation, eu, high, knowledge, regulatory, risk, systems]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [authority, authorized, availability, cooperation, management, procedures] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, categories, compliance, obligations, prohibited, requirements] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.935 (P=0.931, R=0.939). Balanced P/R — the 24-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.1%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.842 > STS 0.718 (gap +0.124). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [act, ai, conformity, documentation, eu] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 46.7%. Shared tokens: [act, ai, conformity, documentation, eu, high, knowledge, regulatory, risk, systems]. EU AI Act-only: [authority, authorized, availability, cooperation, management, procedures]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, categories, compliance, obligations, prohibited, requirements].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.124 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
